Australia’s eastern seaboard is experiencing more frequent extreme rainfall events. This case study demonstrates how integrated hydrologic modeling can shape resilient urban water management.

Challenge

  • Urbanization increased impervious surface; stormwater volume grew.
  • Climate change magnifies flood intensities.

Method

  • Used SWAT for catchment hydrology and HEC-HMS for urban runoff simulation.
  • Incorporated downscaled climate scenarios (RCP 4.5 and 8.5).

Outcome

  • Flood risk maps for 10, 25, and 100-year intervals.
  • Adaptation strategy for local council (green infrastructure, retentions).
  • Policy brief with prioritized interventions.

Key takeaway

Combining advanced hydrologic modeling and policy-ready communication creates actionable climate adaptation in cities.
